solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    vector<int> countBits(int n) {
        vector<int> ans(n + 1, 0);
        for (int i = 1; i <= n; ++i) {
            ans[i] = ans[i >> 1] + (i & 1);
        }
        return ans;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses dynamic programming to calculate the number of 1's in binary representation of each number by reusing previously computed results. For each number i, it uses the count from i/2 and adds 1 if the least significant bit is 1. This approach ensures linear time complexity and avoids any built-in functions.
